Normalise row/column names of a fitZ$mGamma matrix

Source: R/clean.R
normalise_fitZ_names.Rd

A plain multiLCA object uses rownames like "gamma(Intercept|C)" and "gamma(Zp|C)". This function strips the gamma(...) wrapper so names match the clean format used throughout tseLCA ("Intercept", "Zp", etc.) and ensures column names are "C2", "C3", etc.

Usage

normalise_fitZ_names(fitZ, Zp.names = NULL, n_classes = NULL)

Arguments

fitZ

A fitZ-like list with at least $mGamma.

Zp.names

Character vector of covariate column names (used to set clean rownames when the raw names can't be parsed). If NULL, rownames are stripped from the gamma(X|C) pattern only.

n_classes

Integer. Total number of classes (used to derive clean column names if they are non-standard).

Value

fitZ with normalised $mGamma row/col names.
